---
title: "stage_three"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)

# --- 1. The Custom Function ---
# This pulls any World Bank indicator for the whole world
get_world_data <- function(indicator_code) {
  
  # URL for all countries, years 2000-2024
  my_url <- paste0("https://api.worldbank.org/v2/country/all/indicator/", 
                   indicator_code, "?format=json&per_page=17000&date=2000:2024")
  
  # Fetch data
  raw_content <- fromJSON(my_url)
  table_data <- raw_content[[2]]
  
  # Build the tibble
  tibble(
    country = table_data$country$value,
    iso_code = table_data$countryiso3code,
    year = as.numeric(table_data$date),
    value = as.numeric(table_data$value),
    variable = indicator_code
  )
}

# --- 2. Iteration (Acquiring the data) ---
# We use Maternal Mortality and GDP per capita
my_indicators <- c("SH.STA.MMRT", "NY.GDP.PCAP.CD")

# map_df runs the function for both and stacks them
global_raw_data <- map_df(my_indicators, get_world_data)

# --- 3. Tidying the Global Dataset ---
global_study_tidy <- global_raw_data %>%
  # Move indicators into their own columns (This is the "Tidying" requirement)
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = value) %>%
  # Rename for clarity
  rename(
    maternal_mortality = SH.STA.MMRT,
    gdp_per_cap = NY.GDP.PCAP.CD
  ) %>%
  # Remove rows that are missing the main health indicator
  drop_na(maternal_mortality) %>%
  # Remove "World" and "Region" aggregates to keep only actual countries
  # (World Bank aggregate ISOs usually start with digits or are special codes)
  filter(!iso_code %in% c("WLD", "HIC", "MIC", "LIC", "LMY"))

# --- 4. Save and View ---
write_csv(global_study_tidy, "global_maternal_health_study.csv")

# Quick look at the dimensions (should be thousands of rows now!)
print(paste("Total Observations:", nrow(global_study_tidy)))
head(global_study_tidy)
```
